title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Asia-Pacific — Q2 2026
summary: Nine Asia-Pacific markets are operationalizing VASP licensing, custody standards, and AML supervision frameworks through September, creating the region's densest compliance procurement window since Singapore's MiCA-equivalent regime launched. Australia moves from implementation to enforcement on Travel Rule while Korea develops institutional custody infrastructure and Philippines enforces ongoing VASP registration deadlines. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 2026
summary: AMLA becomes operational Q3 with direct supervision authority over Europe's highest-risk crypto firms while the European Commission's MiCA review consultation (31 Aug deadline) determines how DeFi protocols and NFTs fit into the regulatory framework through 2027. Simultaneously, France enforces its 1 July PSAN transition cutoff, UK finalizes authorization rules, and four Middle East markets operationalize licensing regimes Q3-Q4.
